bindings.quest_action: gather enough food to feed the settlement
bindings.quest_stakes: the families who are going hungry
bindings.quest_threat: the famine that's been tightening its grip on us

dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, you have to help us. {quest_stakes} haven't had a proper meal in days.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We need to {quest_action}. I've heard there might be {quest_item} {location_hint}, but none of us are brave enough to make the journey. The {enemy_type} have been prowling the roads and we've already lost two foragers this season.
dialogueChunks.outro: You'd be saving lives, truly. I wouldn't ask if there were any other way.

playerResponses.text: I'll find what you need. Stay safe until I return.
Fine, but I expect fair compensation for the danger.
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.

bindings.quest_stakes: everyone in the settlement who's fallen ill
bindings.quest_threat: a disease that's cutting through us like wildfire
bindings.quest_origin: something that took hold weeks ago without warning

dialogueChunks.intro: Oh thank the stars, a traveler! {player_name}, I beg you, hear me out before you move on.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat}, and we need to {quest_action} {quest_time_pressure}. {target_npc} fell ill first, and now half the settlement can barely stand. The alchemist says we need {quest_item} for a remedy, but it only grows in dangerous territory {location_hint}. I'd go myself but my hands won't stop shaking.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know it's a lot to ask of a stranger. But strangers are all we have left to turn to.

playerResponses.text: Tell me exactly where to look. I'll bring it back.
I'll do it, but only because the children are suffering.
That sounds like a problem for a healer, not me.

bindings.quest_stakes: the settlement's survival through the cold season
bindings.quest_threat: the raiders who have been pillaging the area
bindings.quest_origin: raids that started earlier this season

dialogueChunks.intro: Wait, please don't walk away! I know how this looks, a grown person begging, but I have nowhere else to turn, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} came through two nights ago. Took everything from {quest_focus}: our {quest_item}, our tools, anything they could carry. {target_npc} tried to stop them but they were outnumbered. We tracked them heading {location_hint}, but none of us can fight like you can.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we don't {quest_action} before the cold sets in, {quest_stakes} will be lost. Please.

playerResponses.text: Point me toward their trail. I'll get everything back.
I suppose I could track them down, for the right price.
Chasing raiders isn't something I do for free.

bindings.quest_action: rebuild the mill before the frost comes
bindings.quest_stakes: every family that depends on the mill for grain
bindings.quest_threat: the devastation left by the flood
bindings.quest_origin: the flood that tore through three days ago

dialogueChunks.intro: Forgive me for grabbing your arm like that, {player_name}. I wouldn't do it if I weren't out of options.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_origin} swept away {quest_focus} and half our winter stores. {target_npc} managed to save the youngest children but got hurt doing it and can barely walk now. We need {quest_item} to {quest_action}, but the water drove the {enemy_type} out of the lowlands and they're roaming everywhere. Last I heard, there's a supply {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know you owe us nothing. But without that mill, {quest_stakes} will starve before spring.

playerResponses.text: I'll get what you need. Focus on keeping everyone warm.
Flood damage is rough. I'll help, but I won't do it for free.
There's nothing I can do about a flood. I'm sorry.

bindings.quest_action: retrieve the supplies we abandoned when we fled
bindings.quest_focus: our old home that was overrun
bindings.quest_stakes: the refugee families who barely escaped alive
bindings.quest_threat: the warband that drove us from our home
bindings.quest_origin: the attack that forced us out a week ago

dialogueChunks.intro: You don't know me, {player_name}, and I have no right to burden you with our troubles. But my people are dying.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We're refugees: {quest_origin} left us with nothing but the clothes on our backs. The folk here have been kind, but there isn't enough food or medicine for all of us. {target_npc} sent word that {quest_item} is still stored in {quest_focus} {location_hint}, abandoned when we fled. Nobody will go back: they're too afraid of the {enemy_type} still lurking there.
dialogueChunks.outro: Everything {quest_stakes} need to survive is sitting in a place we're too broken to return to. If you could bring it back, you'd be saving entire families.

playerResponses.text: Tell me the way. I'll bring back everything I can carry.
Heading into overrun territory is serious. What can you offer?
I can't fight an entire warband alone. You need soldiers.

bindings.quest_focus: the dry well and broken pump
bindings.quest_threat: the water crisis that's been choking us for weeks
bindings.quest_origin: the well running dry two weeks ago
bindings.quest_reward_hint: clean water for the whole settlement

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've asked every merchant, every traveler, every guard that's passed through. You're the last chance I have.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} failed us two weeks ago. We've been rationing what little water we had stored, but it ran out yesterday. {target_npc} found an old map showing an underground spring {location_hint}, but the tunnels down there are crawling with {enemy_type}. We sent a small group and only one came back. She said the {quest_item} we need to {quest_action} is down there too, just sitting in the ruins.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} are surviving on puddles, {player_name}. I'll get on my knees if that's what it takes.

playerResponses.text: Stay on your feet. I'll handle the tunnels.
I'll go, but you owe me when this is over.
If your own people couldn't survive down there, neither can I.

bindings.quest_action: get the raw materials to repair our tools
bindings.quest_stakes: the farmers left with broken plows and empty hands
bindings.quest_threat: the loss of every tool we depend on
bindings.quest_origin: the raid that destroyed our workshop

dialogueChunks.intro: I can see you're busy, {player_name}. I wouldn't stop you if lives weren't at stake.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our blacksmith collapsed at the forge yesterday: exhaustion from working day and night trying to repair our tools after {quest_origin}. Without proper tools, we can't harvest, can't hunt, can't defend ourselves. {target_npc} says if we can get {quest_item} from the old mining camp {location_hint}, the blacksmith can {quest_action}. But that camp has been abandoned for good reason.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not warriors or adventurers. We're {quest_stakes}. Please.

playerResponses.text: Point me to the camp. I'll bring back what she needs.
Broken tools and monsters? You really are in trouble. Alright, I'll go.
I'm no errand runner. Find another way.

bindings.quest_action: find our missing courier and recover the shipment
bindings.quest_focus: the abandoned cart on the road
bindings.quest_stakes: the seed grain we need for next season
bindings.quest_threat: the dangers lurking along the trade road

dialogueChunks.intro: Traveler! Wait, please. I saw you walking past and I couldn't let you go without asking.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} went out three days ago to deliver {quest_item} to a neighboring settlement and never arrived. The road {location_hint} has been dangerous lately: {enemy_type} sightings every night. We found {quest_focus} halfway there, wheel broken, supplies scattered. No blood, thank the stars, but no sign of {target_npc} either. We need to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: That delivery was everything we had to trade for {quest_stakes}. Without it, and without {target_npc}, we won't make it to harvest.

playerResponses.text: I'll find {target_npc} and recover the supplies. You have my word.
Missing person and stolen goods? That's two jobs. I expect to be paid for both.
Search parties are not my specialty. I hope they turn up.
